strategy: cuBLAS bf16 GEMM returned as a strided [B,8,S,128] view (transpose/contiguous folded away for free), with bf16 split-K r

sources.content:
import torch

# The GEMM (M=B*S, N=1024, K=5120) is the entire cost: the reshape+transpose to
# [B, 8, S, 128] is a pure stride fold and needs no copy (we return the view,
# deleting the reference's `.contiguous()` round-trip and its kernel launch).
#
# cuBLAS bf16 is at/near practical peak for this shape, so the only remaining
# lever is the GEMM epilogue reduction precision. The tolerance is
# |x-y| <= 1e-5 + 5e-2*|y| at 99% of elements -- enormous next to the ~1-term
# bf16 split-K reduction this enables -- so allowing reduced-precision (bf16)
# accumulation of the split-K partials lets cuBLAS select faster algorithms on
# the compute-bound tail (T >= 2048) at no correctness cost. fp8 is *not* viable
# here: e4m3's ~5% per-element RMS error does not cancel over K and blows the
# 99%@5% gate.
try:
    torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_bf16_reduced_precision_reduction = True
except AttributeError:
    pass


@torch.no_grad()
def run(hidden_states: torch.Tensor, v_proj_weight: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    batch_size, seq_len, _ = hidden_states.shape
    # F.linear -> cuBLAS GEMM: [B, S, 5120] @ [5120, 1024] -> [B, S, 1024]
    value_proj = torch.nn.functional.linear(hidden_states, v_proj_weight)
    # view + transpose are free stride ops; return the strided view (no copy).
    # value_states[b, h, s, d] == value_proj[b, s, h*128 + d], numerically
    # identical to reference.contiguous() but without the extra HBM round-trip.
    return value_proj.view(batch_size, seq_len, 8, 128).transpose(1, 2)

import torch

# The GEMM (M=B*S, N=1024, K=5120) is the entire cost: the reshape+transpose to
# [B, 8, S, 128] is a pure stride fold and needs no copy (we return the view,
# deleting the reference's `.contiguous()` round-trip and its kernel launch).
#
# cuBLAS bf16 is at/near practical peak for this shape, so the only remaining
# lever is the GEMM epilogue reduction precision. The tolerance is
# |x-y| <= 1e-5 + 5e-2*|y| at 99% of elements -- enormous next to the ~1-term
# bf16 split-K reduction this enables -- so allowing reduced-precision (bf16)
# accumulation of the split-K partials lets cuBLAS select faster algorithms on
# the compute-bound tail (T >= 2048) at no correctness cost. fp8 is *not* viable
# here: e4m3's ~5% per-element RMS error does not cancel over K and blows the
# 99%@5% gate.
try:
    torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_bf16_reduced_precision_reduction = True
except AttributeError:
    pass


@torch.no_grad()
def run(hidden_states: torch.Tensor, v_proj_weight: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    batch_size, seq_len, _ = hidden_states.shape
    # F.linear -> cuBLAS GEMM: [B, S, 5120] @ [5120, 1024] -> [B, S, 1024]
    value_proj = torch.nn.functional.linear(hidden_states, v_proj_weight)
    # view + transpose are free stride ops; return the strided view (no copy).
    # value_states[b, h, s, d] == value_proj[b, s, h*128 + d], numerically
    # identical to reference.contiguous() but without the extra HBM round-trip.
    return value_proj.view(batch_size, seq_len, 8, 128).transpose(1, 2)
